title: In-product feedback system
why_it_matters: Grok Build's user guide documents [features] feedback as the feedback system toggle with documented default true. Disabling it at user scope removes the local submission channel for in-product feedback. This rule only observes whether that channel is explicitly disabled or enabled at the user-level config; it does not determine what content any individual feedback submission includes, and other configuration layers are not inspected.

outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config disables the feedback system (features.feedback = false).

outcomes.when: features.feedback is explicitly true in the user-level config
outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config explicitly enables the feedback system (features.feedback = true), the same behavior the user guide documents as default.

remediation.summary: Disable the in-product feedback system at user scope.

outcomes.when: features.feedback is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent
outcomes.message: Effective feedback system posture cannot be determined from an unset user-level value because uninspected configuration layers may supply or override it.
outcomes.unknown_reason: features.feedback is unset in the user-level config; the user guide documents default true, but uninspected CLI flags, environment variables, managed/requirements config, or other layers may determine the effective value.

outcomes.when: features.feedback is set to a value outside boolean true/false
outcomes.message: features.feedback is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed.
outcomes.unknown_reason: features.feedback is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed

sources.title: Grok Build user guide — Configuration
sources.notes: In-tree user guide at OSS SOURCE_REV 124d85bc5dc6e7805560215fcc6d5413944920e1 (retrieved 2026-07-17). Documents [features] feedback = true as "feedback system (default: true)". Local posture only; no wire capture.

limitations: Only the user-level GROK_HOME/config.toml is inspected; CLI flags, environment variables, managed/requirements config, and project-scoped config are not inspected.
An explicit user-level value describes that layer only and does not prove the effective value for every invocation.
This rule does not determine what content any individual feedback submission includes.
This rule cannot confirm any server-side or remote retention of submitted feedback — no local file can.
Auth/account state is user-confirmed-or-unknown; this rule never infers it.
unknown_conditions: Config file is a symlink or non-regular file (not followed).
features.feedback is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent; other configuration layers are not inspected.
features.feedback set to a value outside boolean true/false (raw values are never displayed).
Config file exceeds parse bounds (size > 1 MiB or nesting depth > 32).
